_**User**_

Look at the readme and docs folder to understand this repository. This branch's purpose is to improve the quality of tests for this application. Here are some things I want done - feel free to suggest anything you think might improve tests and lead to fewer errors in the future:

Create a way to get code coverage metrics

Make a clear and visible split between unit, integration, and end-to-end tests
 
Create GitHub Actions that run your tests on every commit. You will need to create two YAML workflow files in the .github/workflows directory. The first, for your frontend code, should be named run-frontend-tests.yml and the second, for your backend code, named run-backend-tests.yml. Each YAML file should check out your code, set up the application environment (e.g., install Node.js if needed), install dependencies, and then execute your tests.

Add tests for anything that's missing them

Do you have any other ideas or best practices that's not being done right now?

---

```json
            "text": "**Configuring frontend tests**"

            "lineNumber": 1054
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1055,
            "text": "Iâm diving into setting up frontend tests. I wonder if I need to look at the Vitest config, but it seems like I might be fine with the defaults. I noticed there's an existing test in &#96;src/app/data/document-repository.test.ts&#96;, so I should check that out. I need to implement React testing, using @testing-library/react and jsdom. Iâll look into package.json for any testing dependencies, and I guess I might need to add the Vitest config in the Vite config. I'll search for setup guidance using Grep.\u003c/details\u003e\u003c/think\u003e"

          },

            "text": "Next Iâll check how frontend tests are currently set up (environment, helpers). Then Iâll add &#96;Login&#96; component tests using the same approach; if the repo doesnât yet have a React DOM test setup, Iâll add the minimal Vitest+jsdom+Testing Library scaffolding needed."

          },

          },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 16292,
            "text": "{"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16293,
            "text": "  \"compilerOptions\": {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16294,
            "text": "    \"target\": \"ES2022\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16295,
            "text": "    \"lib\": [\"ES2022\", \"DOM\", \"DOM.Iterable\"],"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16296,
            "text": "    \"module\": \"ESNext\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16297,
            "text": "    \"skipLibCheck\": true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16298,
            "text": "    \"moduleResolution\": \"bundler\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16299,
            "text": "    \"allowImportingTsExtensions\": true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16300,
            "text": "    \"resolveJsonModule\": true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16301,
            "text": "    \"isolatedModules\": true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16302,
            "text": "    \"noEmit\": true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16303,
            "text": "    \"jsx\": \"react-jsx\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16304,
            "text": "    \"strict\": true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16305,
            "text": "    \"noUnusedLocals\": true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16306,
            "text": "    \"noUnusedParameters\": true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16307,
            "text": "    \"noFallthroughCasesInSwitch\": true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16308,
            "text": "    \"types\": [\"vite/client\", \"@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16309,
            "text": "  },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16310,
            "text": "  \"include\": [\"src\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16311,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16312,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16313
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16314,
            "text": "Wait - &#96;types&#96; in compilerOptions might exclude other types - need to include vitest as well. Let me check - if we set &#96;\"types\": [\"vite/client\", \"@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest\"]&#96; we might omit vitest. Better to use:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16315,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;json"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16316,
            "text": "\"types\": [\"vite/client\", \"vitest/globals\", \"@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16317,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16318
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16319,
            "text": "Or omit &#96;types&#96; and use only triple-slash in vite-env - that way all @types/node etc are included."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16320
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16321,
            "text": "Actually the issue with &#96;types&#96; array is it restricts to ONLY those packages. So we shouldn't set types unless we list everything."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16322
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16323,
            "text": "Best approach: only add to vite-env.d.ts:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16324,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;ts"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16325,
            "text": "/// \u003creference types=\"vite/client\" /\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16326,
            "text": "/// \u003creference types=\"@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest\" /\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16327,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16328
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16329,
            "text": "And ensure tsconfig includes src. If no tsconfig exists, create minimal one without restricting types so that vite-env.d.ts is used."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16330
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16331,
            "text": "Let me check - does Cursor use a tsconfig from parent? The user might have an implicit one."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16332
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16333,
            "text": "I'll add tsconfig.json with:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16334,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;json"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16335,
            "text": "{"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16336,
            "text": "  \"compilerOptions\": {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16337,
            "text": "    \"target\": \"ES2022\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16338,
            "text": "    \"lib\": [\"ES2022\", \"DOM\", \"DOM.Iterable\"],"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16339,
            "text": "    \"module\": \"ESNext\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16340,
            "text": "    \"skipLibCheck\": true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16341,
            "text": "    \"moduleResolution\": \"bundler\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16342,
            "text": "    \"allowImportingTsExtensions\": true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16343,
            "text": "    \"resolveJsonModule\": true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16344,
            "text": "    \"isolatedModules\": true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16345,
            "text": "    \"noEmit\": true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16346,
            "text": "    \"jsx\": \"react-jsx\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16347,
            "text": "    \"strict\": true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16348,
            "text": "  },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16349,
            "text": "  \"include\": [\"src/**/*.ts\", \"src/**/*.tsx\", \"src/**/*.d.ts\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16350,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16351,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16352
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16353,
            "text": "No &#96;types&#96; restriction - so all node_modules/@types are pulled in."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 16354
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 16291
          }
        },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 16057,
            "text": "From jest-dom 6 docs:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16058,
            "text": "For vitest, in setup: &#96;import '@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest'&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16059
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16060,
            "text": "For TypeScript, add to tsconfig:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16061,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;json"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16062,
            "text": "\"types\": [\"@testing-library/jest-dom\", \"vitest/globals\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16063,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16064
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16065,
            "text": "I'll create &#96;tsconfig.json&#96; at root with minimal config that includes types for jest-dom - many Vite templates have this."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16066
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16067,
            "text": "Let me check if there's eslint or other config referencing tsconfig"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16068,
            "text": "\u003c/details\u003e\u003c/think\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16069
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16070,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16071
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16072
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16073
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16074
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16075
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16076
          },
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="create_plan">

```json
{
  "name": "Testing quality improvements",
  "overview": "Add Vitest coverage reporting, reorganize tests into explicit unit/integration/E2E layers, add GitHub Actions for frontend and backend, backfill high-value missing tests, and document conventions—without expanding scope into unrelated refactors.",
  "plan": "# Testing and CI improvement plan\n\n## Current state (from [README.md](README.md) and configs)\n\n- **Frontend:** Vitest + jsdom, tests in [&#96;src/test/**/*.test.{ts,tsx}&#96;](vite.config.ts); [&#96;src/test/setup.ts&#96;](src/test/setup.ts) loads jest-dom.\n- **Backend:** Vitest + node, tests in [&#96;backend/test/**/*.test.ts&#96;](backend/vitest.config.ts); [&#96;coverage.enabled: false&#96;](backend/vitest.config.ts) today.\n- **Mixed styles:** Backend already mixes **pure unit** tests (e.g. [&#96;document-sanitizer.test.ts&#96;](backend/test/document-sanitizer.test.ts), [&#96;document-service.test.ts&#96;](backend/test/document-service.test.ts)) with **HTTP integration** tests (Supertest + [&#96;buildApp&#96;](backend/src/app.js) + in-memory fakes, e.g. [&#96;document-routes.test.ts&#96;](backend/test/document-routes.test.ts)). There is **no E2E** runner (no Playwright/Cypress config in the repo).\n- **CI:** No workflows under &#96;.github/workflows&#96; yet.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Code coverage metrics\n\n**Approach:** Use Vitest’s built-in coverage with the **v8** provider (&#96;@vitest/coverage-v8&#96;), which aligns with Vitest 4.x.\n\n- Add &#96;@vitest/coverage-v8&#96; as a **devDependency** in the **root** [&#96;package.json&#96;](package.json) (frontend) and [&#96;backend/package.json&#96;](backend/package.json).\n- Enable coverage in:\n  - [&#96;vite.config.ts&#96;](vite.config.ts) — &#96;test.coverage&#96; with &#96;provider: 'v8'&#96;, &#96;reporter: ['text', 'lcov', 'json-summary']&#96;, and &#96;reportsDirectory&#96; (e.g. &#96;coverage/frontend&#96;).\n  - [&#96;backend/vitest.config.ts&#96;](backend/vitest.config.ts) — mirror with &#96;reportsDirectory&#96; e.g. &#96;coverage/backend&#96;.\n- Add **npm scripts** (names can match your taste), for example:\n  - Root: &#96;test:frontend:coverage&#96; → &#96;vitest run --coverage&#96;\n  - Backend: &#96;test:backend:coverage&#96; → &#96;vitest run --coverage&#96; (or keep single &#96;test&#96; script and document &#96;--coverage&#96; flag).\n- **Exclude** noise from coverage where appropriate (&#96;coverage.exclude&#96; / &#96;test.coverage.exclude&#96;): e.g. &#96;**/*.d.ts&#96;, build outputs, and optionally &#96;src/test/**&#96; if you only want *product* code measured (team preference).\n- Add **&#96;coverage/&#96;** to [&#96;.gitignore&#96;](.gitignore) if not already present so local/HTML reports are not committed.\n\n**CI (optional enhancement):** Upload &#96;lcov&#96; as an artifact or use Codecov in a follow-up—not required for the first milestone.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Clear split: unit vs integration vs E2E\n\n**Goal:** “Visible” means **directory layout + README + scripts**, not only Vitest tags.\n\n### Recommended layout\n\n| Layer | Meaning in this repo | Location |\n|--------|----------------------|----------|\n| **Unit** | Pure logic, no real HTTP server, no real browser automation; may use mocks/in-memory repos | &#96;src/test/unit/&#96;, &#96;backend/test/unit/&#96; |\n| **Integration** | Frontend: RTL + jsdom component/data tests; Backend: Supertest against in-process Fastify + test doubles | &#96;src/test/integration/&#96;, &#96;backend/test/integration/&#96; |\n| **E2E** | Real browser driving the built app (minimal set to start) | Top-level **&#96;e2e/&#96;** (Playwright convention) |\n\n**Migration work:** Move existing files from flat [&#96;src/test/&#96;](src/test/) and [&#96;backend/test/&#96;](backend/test/) into &#96;unit/&#96; and &#96;integration/&#96; subfolders (helpers stay under &#96;backend/test/helpers/&#96; or &#96;backend/test/integration/helpers/&#96;—pick one and keep imports stable). Update:\n\n- [&#96;vite.config.ts&#96;](vite.config.ts) — &#96;test.include&#96; to cover both subtrees, e.g. &#96;src/test/{unit,integration}/**/*.test.{ts,tsx}&#96;.\n- [&#96;backend/vitest.config.ts&#96;](backend/vitest.config.ts) — &#96;include: [\"test/unit/**/*.test.ts\", \"test/integration/**/*.test.ts\"]&#96; (or equivalent glob).\n\n**Scripts** (root [&#96;package.json&#96;](package.json)) for developer ergonomics:\n\n- &#96;test:frontend:unit&#96; / &#96;test:frontend:integration&#96; — &#96;vitest run&#96; with &#96;--dir&#96; or project-specific config.\n- &#96;test:backend:unit&#96; / &#96;test:backend:integration&#96; — same pattern via Vitest CLI or small **Vitest projects** in one config file.\n\nSimplest implementation: **two Vitest &#96;projects&#96;** in [&#96;vite.config.ts&#96;](vite.config.ts) (frontend) and [&#96;backend/vitest.config.ts&#96;](backend/vitest.config.ts) (backend) so each project has its own &#96;include&#96;/&#96;name&#96; without duplicating full config.\n\n**E2E:** Add **Playwright** at the repo root (&#96;@playwright/test&#96;), config &#96;playwright.config.ts&#96;, and &#96;e2e/*.spec.ts&#96;.\n\n- **Pragmatic first E2E:** **Guest flow smoke** — build or &#96;vite preview&#96;, open app, **Continue as Guest**, assert shell UI (matches README “guest mode” story). No Docker/Keycloak required; fast in CI.\n- **Optional later:** Job that runs &#96;docker compose&#96; + full login E2E (slower, flakier); document as manual or nightly if you want to avoid blocking every PR.\n\nDocument the three layers in [README.md](README.md) **Tests** section (and only if you want a dedicated doc, a short &#96;docs/testing.md&#96;—only add if you explicitly want that file).\n\n---\n\n## 3. GitHub Actions (two workflows as requested)\n\nCreate [&#96;.github/workflows/run-frontend-tests.yml&#96;](.github/workflows/run-frontend-tests.yml) and [&#96;.github/workflows/run-backend-tests.yml&#96;](.github/workflows/run-backend-tests.yml).\n\n**Shared pattern:**\n\n- Triggers: &#96;push&#96; and &#96;pull_request&#96; to main branches (adjust branch names to match your default).\n- **Path filters** (recommended): frontend workflow only when &#96;src/**&#96;, &#96;vite.config.ts&#96;, &#96;package.json&#96;, &#96;package-lock.json&#96; change; backend when &#96;backend/**&#96; changes—reduces noise and cost.\n\n**Frontend workflow:**\n\n- &#96;actions/checkout@v4&#96;\n- &#96;actions/setup-node@v4&#96; with **Node 20**, **cache: npm**\n- &#96;npm ci&#96; at **repo root** (frontend deps + Vitest live there)\n- Run &#96;npm run test:frontend&#96; (and optionally &#96;npm run test:frontend:coverage&#96; + upload artifact)\n\n**Backend workflow:**\n\n- Same Node setup\n- &#96;npm ci&#96; with **&#96;working-directory: backend&#96;** (or &#96;npm ci --prefix backend&#96; from root)\n- Run &#96;npm test&#96; (or &#96;npm run test:backend&#96; from root if you prefer one entrypoint—either is fine if documented)\n\n**Note:** Root [&#96;test:all&#96;](package.json) also runs [&#96;check:font-catalogs&#96;](package.json). Decide whether **font catalog check** belongs in the frontend job (it is a cross-cutting guard) or a separate workflow; minimally, **mention** it in README so contributors don’t assume &#96;test:frontend&#96; is the full gate.\n\n---\n\n## 4. Tests to add (gaps worth closing first)\n\nUse **coverage reports** to drive breadth; prioritize **high-risk, low-coupling** modules.\n\n**Backend (no new DB dependency):**\n\n- **[&#96;backend/src/modules/api-edge/error-handler.ts&#96;](backend/src/modules/api-edge/error-handler.ts)** — map &#96;ApiError&#96; / unknown errors to status + body; pure, fast unit tests in &#96;backend/test/unit/&#96;.\n- **[&#96;backend/src/shared/document-languages.ts&#96;](backend/src/shared/document-languages.ts)** / **[&#96;backend/src/shared/config.ts&#96;](backend/src/shared/config.ts)** — validation or parsing edge cases if logic-heavy.\n- **Optional:** [&#96;jose-token-verifier.ts&#96;](backend/src/modules/identity-access/jose-token-verifier.ts) with **mocked &#96;fetch&#96;/JWKS** (more setup; defer if time-boxed).\n\n**Avoid in first pass (unless you add Testcontainers):** [&#96;pg-document-repository.ts&#96;](backend/src/modules/documents/pg-document-repository.ts), [&#96;db.ts&#96;](backend/src/shared/db.ts) — true integration needs Postgres.\n\n**Frontend:**\n\n- **[&#96;src/app/utils/export.ts&#96;](src/app/utils/export.ts)** / **[&#96;import.ts&#96;](src/app/utils/import.ts)** — unit tests for parsing/serialization boundaries (files are often testable without full Editor).\n- **[&#96;src/app/api/client.ts&#96;](src/app/api/client.ts)** — &#96;fetch&#96; mocked with Vitest, assert headers and error handling.\n- **Editor / autosave** — heavier; add **one** focused integration test for [&#96;useAutoSave&#96;](src/app/hooks/useAutoSave.ts) or a thin wrapper if it keeps tests stable.\n\nStay aligned with [README “Adding a new language font set”](README.md) (font catalog + tests) when touching fonts.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Other best practices (not all mandatory)\n\n- **Typecheck in CI:** Backend already has [&#96;npm run typecheck&#96;](backend/package.json); add a step to &#96;run-backend-tests.yml&#96;. Frontend: add &#96;tsc&#96;/&#96;vite build&#96; if you have a no-emit or build check—only if already cheap.\n- **Coverage thresholds:** Enable &#96;coverage.thresholds&#96; **globally or per-directory** once baselines exist; start conservative to avoid blocking PRs, then tighten.\n- **Branch protection:** Require both workflows (and optionally E2E) on &#96;main&#96;.\n- **Dependabot** for npm: reduces “tests pass locally, CI fails on fresh install” drift.\n- **Determinism:** Keep Supertest apps built once per file (&#96;beforeAll&#96;) as today; avoid shared mutable global state across tests.\n- **E2E scope discipline:** Keep E2E count small; push detailed cases to integration/unit per [testing pyramid](https://martinfowler.com/articles/practical-test-pyramid.html).\n\n---\n\n## Architecture diagram (target test flow)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart TB\n  subgraph local_and_ci [Local and CI]\n    unitFE[src/test/unit Vitest]\n    intFE[src/test/integration Vitest jsdom]\n    unitBE[backend/test/unit Vitest node]\n    intBE[backend/test/integration Supertest]\n    e2e[e2e Playwright browser]\n  end\n  unitFE --\u003e intFE\n  unitBE --\u003e intBE\n  intFE --\u003e e2e\n  intBE --\u003e e2e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Implementation order (suggested)\n\n1. Coverage packages + config + scripts + gitignore.\n2. GitHub Actions (frontend + backend) so the branch gets immediate CI signal.\n3. Folder split + Vitest projects + path updates; run full suite until green.\n4. Playwright + one guest smoke E2E + script &#96;test:e2e&#96; (optional third CI job inside frontend workflow or separate file—**you asked for two YAML files**, so either run E2E inside &#96;run-frontend-tests.yml&#96; as an extra job or add a note that a third workflow is optional).\n5. Backfill unit tests for error handler + client/export/import as time allows.\n\n**Open choice:** Running E2E inside **&#96;run-frontend-tests.yml&#96;** as a second &#96;job&#96; (same file) keeps the repo to **two workflow files** while still running browser tests on every change to the app.\n",
```

Here is what was implemented:

### Coverage
- Added `@vitest/coverage-v8` to the root and [`backend/package.json`](backend/package.json).
- Configured coverage in [`vite.config.ts`](vite.config.ts) (`coverage/frontend`) and [`backend/vitest.config.ts`](backend/vitest.config.ts) (`coverage/backend`), with sensible excludes.
- Scripts: `test:frontend:coverage`, `test:backend:coverage` (and backend `test:coverage`).
- `.gitignore` already had `coverage/`; added Playwright output dirs.

### Unit / integration layout
- **Frontend:** [`src/test/unit/`](src/test/unit/) (e.g. `auth`, `folder-utils`, `signup-validation`, `editor-selection`, new `api-client`, `export`, `import`) and [`src/test/integration/`](src/test/integration/) (RTL tests and `document-repository`).
- **Backend:** [`backend/test/unit/`](backend/test/unit/) vs [`backend/test/integration/`](backend/test/integration/); [`backend/test/helpers/`](backend/test/helpers/) unchanged; integration tests use `../../src/...` and `../helpers/...`.
- [`backend/test/unit/migrations-definition.test.ts`](backend/test/unit/migrations-definition.test.ts) resolves migrations via `../../migrations`.

### GitHub Actions
- [`run-frontend-tests.yml`](.github/workflows/run-frontend-tests.yml): Node 20, `npm ci`, font catalog check, Vitest with coverage, artifact upload, **second job** for Playwright (Chromium install + `npm run test:e2e`). Path filters include `e2e/**` and `playwright.config.ts`.
- [`run-backend-tests.yml`](.github/workflows/run-backend-tests.yml): `working-directory: backend`, `typecheck`, `test:coverage`, artifact upload.

### E2E
- [`playwright.config.ts`](playwright.config.ts) — `vite build` + `vite preview` on port 4173.
- [`e2e/guest-smoke.spec.ts`](e2e/guest-smoke.spec.ts) — guest flow → “Welcome to GlossaDocs”.
- Scripts: `test:e2e`, `test:e2e:install`.

### New tests
- [`backend/test/unit/error-handler.test.ts`](backend/test/unit/error-handler.test.ts) — `ApiError`, Zod, and generic errors.
- [`src/test/unit/api-client.test.ts`](src/test/unit/api-client.test.ts) — `getApiBaseUrl`, `apiRequest` (mocked `fetch`).
- [`src/test/unit/import.test.ts`](src/test/unit/import.test.ts) — `isSupportedDocumentFile` and unsupported `importDocumentFile`.
- [`src/test/unit/export.test.ts`](src/test/unit/export.test.ts) — invalid `exportDocument` format, `exportAsJson` download path.

### Docs
- [`README.md`](README.md) — test layers, commands, CI, updated font-test paths.

`npm run test:all` completes successfully (49 frontend + 100 backend tests).


### Change

`defineConfig` is now imported from `vitest/config` instead of `vite`.

### Verification

- No issues reported for `vite.config.ts` in the IDE linter.
- `npx vite build` completes successfully.

### Why this fixes it

`vite`’s `defineConfig` is typed as Vite-only `UserConfig`, which does not include a `test` field. `vitest/config`’s `defineConfig` extends that type with Vitest’s options, so the `test` block (and coverage) is valid for TypeScript. Runtime behavior is unchanged: it’s still the same merged Vite + Vitest config API.

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


### Fix

Added a root [`tsconfig.json`](c:\Users\tommy\CS485\GlossaDocs\tsconfig.json) with `baseUrl: "."` and `paths`: `"@/*": ["./src/*"]`, plus settings aligned with Vite/React/Vitest (`moduleResolution: "Bundler"`, `jsx`, `types` for `vite/client` and `vitest/globals`, `include` for `src` and config files).

### Verification

- IDE diagnostics: no issues on `signup-validation.test.ts`.
- `npx vitest run src/test/unit/signup-validation.test.ts` passes (3 tests).

### Why it broke

`@/` is only a **Vite** resolve alias. Without a **TypeScript** `paths` mapping, the language service does not map `@/app/...` to `src/app/...`, so it reports “Cannot find module”. The new `tsconfig.json` mirrors the Vite alias so TypeScript can resolve those imports.

---

Fix it, verify, and then give a concise explanation. @src/test/unit/api-client.test.ts:58-61

### Change

Introduced a `jsonResponse()` helper that builds the small object your tests need (`ok`, `status`, `json`) and asserts it as `Response`. All `mockResolvedValue` / `mockResolvedValueOnce` calls now go through that helper.

### Verification

- No linter issues on `api-client.test.ts`.
- `npx vitest run src/test/unit/api-client.test.ts` — 8 tests passed.
- `npx tsc --noEmit` no longer reports errors for this file.

### Why

`fetch` is typed to return a full DOM `Response`, which has many required fields. Plain object literals only implement what `apiRequest` uses, so TypeScript correctly complained. Casting that minimal stub to `Response` documents that it’s intentional test double, not a real browser response.

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="create_plan">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **create_plan**</summary>


Parameters:

```json
{
  "name": "Coverage-driven test quality",
  "overview": "Use coverage reports as a compass (trends and hotspots), then add behavior-focused tests in the highest-risk gaps—especially auth/config and client session hydration—while avoiding shallow “line stuffing” and deferring DB-heavy paths unless you invest in real infrastructure tests.",
  "plan": "# Plan: Improve test quality using coverage as a signal\n\n## How to use coverage (indicator, not gospel)\n\n1. **Baseline:** Run [&#96;npm run test:frontend:coverage&#96;](package.json) and [&#96;npm run test:backend:coverage&#96;](package.json) locally. Open &#96;coverage/frontend/lcov-report/index.html&#96; and &#96;coverage/backend/lcov-report/index.html&#96; (or scan &#96;coverage-summary.json&#96;) to see **statements/branches** per file.\n2. **Interpret:** High coverage on trivial code (thin pass-through components) is misleading. Prefer **branch coverage** and **risk weighting** (auth, persistence, money/data loss) over raw percentages.\n3. **Track over time:** Re-run after meaningful refactors; watch for **drops** in critical modules, not chasing 100% everywhere.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart LR\n  coverage[Coverage hotspots]\n  risk[Risk and complexity]\n  tests[New tests]\n  coverage --\u003e risk\n  risk --\u003e tests\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Critical gaps (why they matter)\n\n| Area | File(s) | Why subpar today |\n|------|---------|------------------|\n| **Env / boot** | [&#96;backend/src/shared/config.ts&#96;](backend/src/shared/config.ts) | Large Zod schema (&#96;booleanFromEnv&#96;, ports, OIDC fields). Misconfiguration can fail at runtime or silently mis-set cookies. **No dedicated unit tests.** |\n| **JWT verification (production path)** | [&#96;backend/src/modules/identity-access/jose-token-verifier.ts&#96;](backend/src/modules/identity-access/jose-token-verifier.ts) | Integration tests use a **fake** [&#96;TokenVerifier&#96;](backend/test/integration/document-routes.test.ts); real &#96;jwtVerify&#96; + claim mapping is **never exercised** in CI. |\n| **Session bootstrap (frontend)** | [&#96;src/app/utils/auth.ts&#96;](src/app/utils/auth.ts) (&#96;getAuthenticatedUserFromBackend&#96;) | Subtle branches: guest short-circuit, successful &#96;/auth/session&#96;, &#96;ApiClientError&#96; vs non-API errors, localStorage clears. High bug cost if wrong. |\n| **Autosave timing** | [&#96;src/app/hooks/useAutoSave.ts&#96;](src/app/hooks/useAutoSave.ts) | Timer + ref behavior; easy to regress with a one-line change. Small surface, **high value** with fake timers. |\n| **Keycloak admin HTTP** | [&#96;backend/src/modules/identity-access/keycloak-admin-client.ts&#96;](backend/src/modules/identity-access/keycloak-admin-client.ts) | Many &#96;fetch&#96; branches and [&#96;KeycloakAdminClientError&#96;](backend/src/modules/identity-access/keycloak-admin-client.ts) codes. Route tests **inject** a mock client, so **this module’s HTTP logic is untested** in CI. |\n\n**Lower priority for this pass (unless you add infra):** [&#96;pg-document-repository.ts&#96;](backend/src/modules/documents/pg-document-repository.ts), [&#96;db.ts&#96;](backend/src/shared/db.ts), [&#96;redis-auth-session-store.ts&#96;](backend/src/modules/identity-access/redis-auth-session-store.ts) — need Postgres/Redis or Testcontainers; perfunctory mocks often **lie** about SQL and ordering.\n\n---\n\n## Proposed work (meaningful tests first)\n\n### 1. Backend: &#96;getConfig&#96; / env schema ([&#96;config.ts&#96;](backend/src/shared/config.ts))\n\n- **Unit tests** in [&#96;backend/test/unit/&#96;](backend/test/unit/) (new file, e.g. &#96;config.test.ts&#96;).\n- **Assert real behavior:** invalid env produces a thrown error with a message that includes Zod issues; &#96;AUTH_SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE&#96; accepts &#96;\"true\"&#96; / &#96;\"1\"&#96; / &#96;\"yes\"&#96; and rejects garbage consistently; numeric coercion for &#96;API_PORT&#96; / &#96;AUTH_SESSION_TTL_SECONDS&#96;.\n- **Why not perfunctory:** If someone changes &#96;booleanFromEnv&#96; or defaults, tests **fail** and catch production misconfiguration early.\n\n### 2. Backend: &#96;JoseTokenVerifier&#96; ([&#96;jose-token-verifier.ts&#96;](backend/src/modules/identity-access/jose-token-verifier.ts))\n\n- **Unit tests** with **&#96;vi.mock(\"jose\")&#96;** (or partial mock of &#96;jwtVerify&#96;) returning controlled payloads.\n- **Cases worth locking in:** missing &#96;sub&#96; → [&#96;ApiError&#96;](backend/src/shared/api-error.ts) &#96;AUTH_INVALID_SUB&#96;; &#96;preferred_username&#96; / &#96;name&#96; / &#96;email&#96; / &#96;scope&#96; mapping; non-&#96;ApiError&#96; from &#96;jwtVerify&#96; → wrapped as &#96;AUTH_INVALID_TOKEN&#96;.\n- **Why not perfunctory:** Exercises the **real class** and claim mapping; failures mean JWT identity in production would be wrong.\n\n### 3. Frontend: &#96;getAuthenticatedUserFromBackend&#96; ([&#96;auth.ts&#96;](src/app/utils/auth.ts))\n\n- **Unit tests** with &#96;vi.mock(\"@/app/api/endpoints\")&#96; (or mock &#96;authApi.session&#96;).\n- **Cases:** guest / no user returns without calling API; successful session updates storage; &#96;ApiClientError&#96; clears user; non-API error clears user and does not throw (matches “avoid broken authenticated state” comment in code).\n- **Why not perfunctory:** These are **state-machine** assertions; they fail if someone “simplifies” error handling and breaks logout or session recovery.\n\n### 4. Frontend: &#96;useAutoSave&#96; ([&#96;useAutoSave.ts&#96;](src/app/hooks/useAutoSave.ts))\n\n- **Unit test** with **&#96;vi.useFakeTimers()&#96;** ([Vitest timer API](https://vitest.dev/guide/mocking.html#timers)): after &#96;AUTO_SAVE_DELAY_MS&#96;, &#96;onSave&#96; runs once; toggling &#96;hasUnsavedChanges&#96; clears/resets timer; unmount clears timeout.\n- **Why not perfunctory:** Directly guards editor data-loss behavior.\n\n### 5. Backend (optional but high value): &#96;keycloak-admin-client&#96; HTTP paths\n\n- **Unit tests** with **mocked &#96;globalThis.fetch&#96;** sequences (admin token, then create user / reset password).\n- **Assert:** HTTP failures map to [&#96;KeycloakAdminClientError&#96;](backend/src/modules/identity-access/keycloak-admin-client.ts) codes you care about (e.g. user exists, admin unavailable), not just “some error.”\n- **Caveat:** Brittle if Keycloak API shape changes—treat as **contract tests**; update when API changes. Still better than zero coverage on fetch error branches.\n\n### 6. Documentation touchpoint\n\n- Add a short subsection to [&#96;docs/testing.md&#96;](docs/testing.md): “Using coverage” (run commands, read reports, prioritize risk). Keeps [&#96;README.md&#96;](README.md) thin.\n\n---\n\n## What to skip or defer (avoid false comfort)\n\n- **Huge PDF/html2pdf paths** in [&#96;export.ts&#96;](src/app/utils/export.ts) unless you extract pure helpers and test those—full E2E is more honest than mocked canvas.\n- **Snapshot-only** tests of large component trees without behavior assertions.\n- **100% on** [&#96;lambda.ts&#96;](backend/src/lambda.ts) **or** [&#96;server.ts&#96;](backend/src/server.ts) unless you add targeted integration harness—often low ROI.\n\n---\n\n## Suggested order of implementation\n\n1. &#96;config.test.ts&#96; (fast, no mocks).\n2. &#96;JoseTokenVerifier&#96; with mocked &#96;jose&#96;.\n3. &#96;auth.ts&#96; session hydration tests.\n4. &#96;useAutoSave&#96; fake timers.\n5. &#96;keycloak-admin-client&#96; fetch mocks (if time).\n6. &#96;docs/testing.md&#96; coverage note.\n\n---\n\n## Optional question (only if you want Postgres/Redis in scope)\n\nIf you want repository-level tests in the same initiative, that implies **Testcontainers** (or a dedicated CI service) and a different time budget. Say so and the plan can add a **Phase B** for &#96;pg-document-repository&#96; + migrations smoke—otherwise keep Phase B out of scope to avoid shallow SQL mocks.\n",
```

Summary of what was implemented:

### Documentation ([docs/testing.md](docs/testing.md))
- **Using coverage** — how to run reports, read HTML/`coverage-summary`, and use them as a signal (not a single score).
- **What we do not fake** — Postgres/Redis integration tests deferred unless you add real infra (e.g. Testcontainers).

### Backend unit tests
- **[backend/test/unit/config.test.ts](backend/test/unit/config.test.ts)** — `getConfig`: defaults, numeric coercion, `AUTH_SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE` strings, invalid env (bad boolean, `NODE_ENV`, `API_PORT`), `redis` store.
- **[backend/test/unit/jose-token-verifier.test.ts](backend/test/unit/jose-token-verifier.test.ts)** — `vi.hoisted` + `vi.mock("jose")`: claim mapping, username fallbacks, `AUTH_INVALID_SUB`, `ApiError` passthrough, `AUTH_INVALID_TOKEN` for generic errors.
- **[backend/test/unit/keycloak-admin-client.test.ts](backend/test/unit/keycloak-admin-client.test.ts)** — `requireKeycloakAdminConfig`; `HttpKeycloakAdminClient`: failed token request, missing `access_token`, fetch rejection, `409` → `KEYCLOAK_USER_EXISTS`, empty search → `KEYCLOAK_USER_NOT_FOUND`.

### Frontend unit tests
- **[src/test/unit/auth.test.ts](src/test/unit/auth.test.ts)** — `getAuthenticatedUserFromBackend`: guest skips `fetch`, successful `/auth/session` refreshes `localStorage`, `fetch` rejection clears storage.
- **[src/test/unit/useAutoSave.test.ts](src/test/unit/useAutoSave.test.ts)** — fake timers: delayed save, no save when clean, cancel when `dirty` flips off, unmount clears pending timer.

### Verification
- `npm run test:frontend` — 56 tests passed.  
- `npm run test:backend` — 126 tests passed.  

All related todos are marked completed.

---

_**User**_

There is a bug where saved pdfs include the title of the document. The pdf contents should only include the text that has been typed in the document.

Create tests so we can do test-driven development, but only write as much code as necessary to get the test to fail.

Test: Have a document with a title
Save the document
Make sure the document has the correct text
Try with different title and any edge cases

---

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


Here’s what was added and how it behaves.

### What changed

1. **`buildPdfRenderContainer` in [`src/app/utils/export.ts`](src/app/utils/export.ts)**  
   The PDF DOM (root + title `h1` + body `div`) is built in one place and **`exportAsPdf` reuses it** (same behavior as before). This avoids pulling in `html2pdf` in tests (which was hanging the suite).

2. **New [`src/test/unit/export-pdf.test.ts`](src/test/unit/export-pdf.test.ts)**  
   Four tests describe the desired behavior: PDF **visible text** should only reflect **editor body** HTML—**no** document title, **no** `"Untitled Document"` fallback in the page, and **no** `h1.pdf-title` for whitespace-only titles.

### Verification

```bash
npx vitest run src/test/unit/export-pdf.test.ts
```

**All 4 tests fail (red)** against the current implementation, which still prepends the title.

### Why this shape

- **TDD red:** Assertions encode the bug: title strings are still present in `textContent` / the title node still exists.  
- **Minimal production code:** Only a small extract + reuse of `exportAsPdf`; no PDF fix yet.  
- **No `html2pdf` in tests:** Testing the DOM we pass to `html2pdf` is enough for this bug and avoids slow/hanging loads.

**Next step (green):** In `buildPdfRenderContainer`, stop appending the title `h1` (and drop any `"Untitled Document"` fallback in the PDF), then rerun these tests until they pass.

_**User**_

Is it possible to make the frontend and backend tests show their results in a single output? Right now, it runs the frontend tests, show the results in the terminal, then runs the backend tests and shows its results. I'm worried the frontend tests might fail in the future and I won't see in the terminal because the backend tests will cover it. Ideally I just get one report that says XX tests run, XX tests passed, XX tests failed. If this is too complicated don't bother, just curious

---

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Setting up vitest workspace**

I'm configuring a Vitest workspace to run frontend and backend tests together and aggregate results. I'm exploring the root vitest workspace approach with projects for frontend and backend configs. I'm implementing a vitest workspace with separate projects for frontend and backend using vitest.workspace.ts. 

**Configuring vitest workspace**

I'm adjusting the vitest workspace config to handle backend tests with relative includes. </details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


Yes, it’s possible, and it doesn’t have to be very complicated.

### Why you see two blocks today
`test:all` runs **two separate processes** (`vitest` in the repo root, then `vitest` in `backend/`). Each process prints its own summary, so you never get a single “N tests total” line from npm alone.

### Practical approach: one Vitest run (workspace)
Vitest supports a **workspace** so a single `vitest run` can load **multiple projects** (your frontend config + your backend config), each with its own `environment` (e.g. `jsdom` vs `node`). At the end you get **one** run with a **combined** summary (and one exit code for the whole run).

Rough shape:

- Add something like `vitest.workspace.ts` at the repo root that lists both projects (often `./vite.config.ts` and `./backend/vitest.config.ts`, sometimes with `root` / `test.root` for the backend so paths stay correct).
- Point `npm run test:all` at that single command (e.g. `vitest run` with workspace), and keep `test:frontend` / `test:backend` as shortcuts to `--project` filters if you still want to run one side only.

Caveats: you need to make sure **include paths, `root`, and coverage output dirs** still make sense when both projects run from the repo root (usually a small round of config tweaks).

### Alternatives (usually worse)
- **Piping two JSON reports** into a custom merger script: doable, but more moving parts.
- **Concurrently**: two processes again; output is harder to read, not really “one report.”

So: **not too complicated** if you’re okay adjusting npm scripts and adding a workspace file; that’s the usual way to get one terminal summary for frontend + backend Vitest. If you want this wired up in the repo, say so and we can do it carefully without breaking CI.

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


Here’s what changed:

### New workflow: [`.github/workflows/check-font-catalogs.yml`](.github/workflows/check-font-catalogs.yml)
- Triggers on **push** and **pull_request** targeting **`main`** and **`develop`** (no path filters, so it **always** runs on those events).
- Single job: checkout → Node 20 → `node scripts/check-font-catalogs.mjs` (no `npm ci`; the script only reads the two TS files).
- Shows up in GitHub as its own check, e.g. **“Font catalogs / match”**.

### Frontend workflow
- Removed the duplicate font step and the `scripts/check-font-catalogs.mjs` path entry so font checks aren’t duplicated when both workflows run.

### Docs
- [`docs/testing.md`](docs/testing.md) — CI section updated for `main`/`develop` and the new workflow.
- [`README.md`](README.md) — short CI blurb points at `docs/testing.md`.

**Why a separate workflow:** The frontend workflow is **path-filtered**, so a PR that only changes `backend/src/shared/document-fonts.ts` would not run frontend tests and would previously **skip** the font check there. The new workflow always validates the catalogs on PRs to `main`/`develop`.

DO NOT write perfunctory tests for the sole purpose of increasing coverage, write real tests that might catch real bugs. Increasing coverage should be a side effect, we're just using because it's the simplest metric to use.

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


The prompt didn’t “break”; the last run stopped mid-fix. Here’s what was wrong and what we finished.

### What went wrong
1. **`vi.clearAllMocks()` + `isAuthenticatedMode`** — Clearing mocks removed the default `() => true` implementation, so `isAuthenticatedMode()` returned `undefined` (falsy). The repository always took the **guest** path, so `foldersApi.create` was never called and “auth” tests failed.
2. **Guest tests + real IndexedDB** — `Promise.all` loaded `document-repository` in parallel with `@/app/utils/db`. The repository sometimes bound to the **real** `db` module before the mocked one was the one used for that graph, so `indexedDB` blew up in jsdom.

### What we changed
- **`restoreAuthenticatedSessionMock()`** — After every `resetModules` + `clearAllMocks`, auth suites call `vi.mocked(isAuthenticatedMode).mockReturnValue(true)` again.
- **Guest describes** — `beforeEach` sets `mockReturnValue(false)` so guest mode is explicit and stable.
- **Guest tests** — Import **`@/app/utils/db` first**, then **`@/app/data/document-repository`**, so the mocked DB is what `document-repository` closes over.
- **More behavior-focused tests** — Authenticated `updateFolder` / `deleteFolder`, guest `updateFolder`, plain `{ status: 404 }` on update (exercises **`getApiErrorStatus`**), and non-404 update failure.

### Status
- **`npm run test:frontend`** — **85 tests**, all passing.
- **Coverage** (with `--coverage.include` for those two files): **`document-repository.ts` ~96% lines**, **`keyboardLayouts.ts` 100% lines** — both **above 80%** on lines.

Files involved: `src/test/integration/document-repository.test.ts`, `src/test/unit/keyboard-layouts.test.ts`, and `src/app/data/document-repository.ts` ( **`getApiErrorStatus`** + using it for 404 handling from the earlier step).

---

_**User**_

Write one English-language test specification for each of the two critical files in the frontend we just wrote tests for. Each specification should contain a list of all functions in the code file, followed by a table of tests. Each row of the table should describe the purpose of the test, the test inputs to the function, and the test output that is expected if the test passes. If there's not at least one unit test for every function, write one. Put these files in docs/specs/test/ 

---

Here’s what was added and updated.

### New specification files (`docs/specs/test/`)

1. **`docs/specs/test/document-repository.test-spec.md`**  
   - Lists **all exported** repository functions plus **internal** helpers (`getApiErrorStatus`, `isUuid`, `toAppDocument`, `toAppFolder`).  
   - **Test table** with one row per automated test in `src/test/integration/document-repository.test.ts`: purpose, functions under test, inputs (mocks/arguments), and expected outcome.  
   - Short **coverage note**: every exported function has at least one row; internals are only exercised through those tests.

2. **`docs/specs/test/keyboard-layouts.test-spec.md`**  
   - Lists **exported** functions (`getKeyboardLayout`, `getRemappedCharacter`) and **internal** helpers (`key`, `shouldUseShiftedCharacter`, `buildRemapTable`).  
   - **Test table** aligned with `src/test/unit/keyboard-layouts.test.ts`, including the new case-insensitivity case.  
   - **Coverage note** on how internals are covered and that no layout key sets an explicit `shiftOutput` today.

### Code change (so the keyboard spec stays accurate)

- **`src/test/unit/keyboard-layouts.test.ts`** — one extra test: English `key: "Q"` maps like `"q"` so **`buildRemapTable`**’s lowercase `typedWith` keys and **`getRemappedCharacter`**’s `key.toLowerCase()` behavior are explicitly tested.

`document-repository` internals are already exercised by the integration tests (404 handling, UUID gates, API mapping); no new tests were required beyond what you already had.

---

_**User**_

Ok, now let's do the same for the backend. Look through the source code of the backend and identify two code files that contain the most core functionality that implements the two backend user stories. There must be at least 5 functions in each file.

Write one English-language test specification for each code file you plan to test. Each specification should contain a list of all functions in the code file, followed by a table of tests. Each row of the table should describe the purpose of the test, the test inputs to the function, and the test output that is expected if the test passes. You must write at least one unit test for every function.

Create the tests and check if 80% coverage has been achieved for each file. If not, go back and write more meaningful tests.

Coverage is only a metric - prefer failing tests that actually indicate something over passing tests that are just there to inflate coverage metrics.

---

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_

Exploring the backend codebase and user stories to identify the two most critical files, then adding tests and specifications.

**Handling edge cases**

I'm implementing early returns and 404 assertions for edge cases in folder update operations. I'm implementing test cases for edge cases in folder update operations. I'm adding test cases for edge cases in folder update operations. I confirmed that EmptyRepository.findOwnedById always returns null, which is expected behavior. I'm adding tests for the deleteOwned method to verify its return value. I'm verifying that `deleteOwned` returns `false` as expected when called on an empty repository. 

I'm adding tests for `createFolder` edge cases including missing parent folders. I'm adding tests for `updateFolder` edge cases including self-parent folder updates. I'm correcting the test to ensure at least one field is provided for updates, avoiding empty update scenarios. I'm updating the test to verify that clearing a folder ID works correctly. I'm adding tests for Keycloak admin client error handling and user creation flows. 

I'm investigating the `isKeycloakAdminErrorCode` function bug and adding tests for missing location header in createUser. </details></think>

## Files chosen (≥5 functions each)

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| **`backend/src/modules/documents/document-service.ts`** | Core **document/folder** rules for authenticated users (Story 1 backend): sanitization, font checks, folder ownership, empty updates, cycle detection. |
| **`backend/src/modules/identity-access/keycloak-admin-client.ts`** | **Keycloak Admin API** client for registration/password-reset style flows: token, `createUser`, `sendPasswordResetEmail`, config guard, error typing. |

## Specifications (`docs/specs/test/`)

- **`document-service.test-spec.md`** — Full function list (exported + private helpers), test table (purpose, inputs, expected outcome), coverage note.  
- **`keycloak-admin-client.test-spec.md`** — Same for the Keycloak client + link fix to `auth.ts`.

## Tests added/extended

- **`backend/test/unit/document-service.test.ts`** — Delegation tests (`listByOwner`, `listFolders`, `getOwned`, `getOwnedFolder`, `deleteOwned`, `deleteFolder`), `createFolder` parent validation, `updateFolder` success paths (rename + reparent), validation cases, `updateOwned` folder/font/language behavior.  
- **`backend/test/unit/keycloak-admin-client.test.ts`** — `isKeycloakAdminErrorCode`, full `createUser` / `sendPasswordResetEmail` success paths, more failure modes, `keycloakRequest` null-response path, email local-part edge case for `firstName`.
